extraction.one_line_summary: DEMARE Arnaud won a chaotic sprint after CAVENDISH crashed into barriers following contact with SAGAN Peter at 200m to go.
tactical_patterns: FDJ rider returned to leadout so fast that when Demare dove sideways, following riders couldn't react, causing first crash 1.5k out.
Cavendish committed to barrier-side gap at 200m that closed, suggesting sprinters gamble on disappearing spaces when desperate for positioning.
Two crashes in final 1.5k meant fewer sprinters contested finish, suggesting early positioning crashes can accidentally benefit those who navigate cleanly.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: attempted gap against barriers that wasn't there at 200m, showing desperation from poor positioning or declining sprint instincts
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: body motion deemed extreme by commentators and team manager called it 'act of violence,' facing potential Tour disqualification
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: positive: won but team manager suggested he'd want another sprint win to prove fastest without chaos intervening
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: neutral: started sprint from too far back and wouldn't have beaten Demare even without crashes
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: secured third despite chaos, riding well through dangerous finale
